title: MIDI PianoMotion Tools V1.50
description:
MIDI PianoMotion – Transform MIDI Files into Stunning Piano Animations in Blender

Overview

Unlock the full potential of your musical creations with MIDI PianoMotion, the premier Blender plugin meticulously crafted to convert your MIDI files into lifelike piano animations. Whether you're a music producer, composer, educator, or a visual artist, MIDI PianoMotion streamlines the animation process, allowing you to effortlessly add dynamic visual elements to your projects.

Pro Version：https://superhivemarket.com/products/midi-piano-motion-tools-pro-max---transform-music-into-visual-magic





Key Features

1. Automatic Piano Animation Generation

  • Seamless MIDI Import: Easily import your MIDI files into Blender and let MIDI PianoMotion handle the rest.
  • Realistic Performance: Automatically generates authentic piano animations that mirror your MIDI compositions with precision.

2. Programmatic Binding for Ultimate Realism

  • Detailed Dynamics: Achieves comprehensive programmatic binding of piano components, including keys, pedals, and hammers.
  • Smooth Animations: Ensures fluid and natural movements, enhancing the realism of each performance.

3. Built-In High-Quality Piano Model

  • Ready-to-Use: Comes with a meticulously designed piano model, eliminating the need for additional modeling.
  • Customizable Design: Modify the built-in model to match your specific aesthetic preferences or project requirements.

4. Diverse Animation Templates

  • Variety of Styles: Choose from multiple animation styles to suit different creative needs, whether it's for a music video, educational content, or interactive media.
  • Template Customization: Easily tweak templates to create unique animations tailored to your vision.

5. Real-Time Preview and Editing

  • Instant Feedback: View animation changes in real-time, allowing for quick adjustments and fine-tuning.
  • User-Friendly Interface: Intuitive controls make it easy to navigate and modify animation parameters without a steep learning curve.

6. High Compatibility and Integration

  • Wide Format Support: Compatible with various MIDI formats, ensuring flexibility in your workflow.
  • Blender Versions: Supports multiple versions of Blender, providing seamless integration regardless of your setup.

Benefits

  • Enhance Visual Appeal: Elevate your musical projects with visually captivating piano animations that complement your audio.
  • Save Time and Effort: Automate the complex process of piano animation creation, allowing you to focus on your creative endeavors.
  • Versatile Applications: Ideal for creating demonstration videos, educational materials, social media content, game assets, and more.
  • Professional Quality: Deliver high-quality animations that meet industry standards, impressing clients and audiences alike.
  • Ease of Use: Designed for both beginners and professionals, ensuring a smooth and efficient animation process.

Installation and Usage

  1. Download and Install

    • Purchase and download MIDI PianoMotion from Blender Market.
    • In Blender, navigate to Edit > Preferences > Add-ons > Install, and select the downloaded ZIP file.
    • Enable the add-on by checking the box next to MIDI PianoMotion in the Add-ons list.

  2. Importing MIDI Files

    • Open your Blender project and access the MIDI PianoMotion panel.
    • Click on Import MIDI and select your desired MIDI file.

  3. Generate Animation

    • Once imported, the plugin will automatically generate the piano animation based on your MIDI data.
    • Use the real-time preview to adjust parameters such as animation speed, style, and dynamics.

  4. Customize and Export

    • Modify the built-in piano model or apply different animation templates as needed.
    • Export your animation in your preferred format for use in videos, presentations, or interactive applications.

Compatibility

  • Blender Versions: Compatible with Blender 3.0 and above.
  • Operating Systems: Supports Windows, macOS, and Linux.
  • MIDI Formats: Supports standard MIDI files (.mid, .midi).

Support and Updates

  • Comprehensive Documentation: Access detailed user manuals and tutorials to help you get started and make the most of MIDI PianoMotion.
  • Regular Updates: Benefit from continuous improvements, new features, and bug fixes to ensure optimal performance.
  • Dedicated Support: Reach out to our support team for any questions or assistance needed. We're here to help you succeed!

documentation:
PianoMotion

User Manual

Plugin Overview

MIDI PianoMotion Tools is a specialized addon for Blender designed to convert MIDI files into piano animations.
It generates realistic keypress animations, hammer actions, and pedal effects, and supports customizable emission effects to enhance visual performance.

System Requirements

  • Blender 4.2.0 or later

  • Python library dependency: mido (bundled installation instructions provided)

Features

MIDI File Import

  • Supports standard MIDI file formats

  • Automatically converts MIDI events into piano animations

Animation Effects

  • Realistic keypress animations

  • Hammer mechanism animations

  • Three-pedal animation effects

  • Left and right hand color differentiation

Customization Options

  • Adjustable key colors

  • Flexible key naming rules

  • Adjustable animation parameters

Installation

2.1 Add-on Installation

Install the add-on via Blender Preferences from a .zip file:

  1. Obtain the .zip file from the website or developer, e.g., MIDI_PianoMotion_Tools.zip.

  2. In Blender, go to Edit > Preferences > Add-ons.

  3. Click Install, select the .zip file, and click Install Add-on.

  4. In the Add-ons list, find PianoMotion Pro and enable it by checking the checkbox.

2.2 Mido Library Dependency

mido is a Python library used for handling MIDI files. PianoMotion Pro requires mido to function properly.

If you encounter an error like No module named 'mido', it means the library is missing.

Solution:

In Blender’s Python Console, run:




import pip





pip.main(['install', 'mido'])

Alternatively:

  • Install mido using your system’s Python installation.

  • Copy the installed mido module into Blender’s scripts/modules folder.

Then restart Blender. If successful, MIDI file importing will work correctly.

After installation, press N in the 3D Viewport and go to the Piano tab to access the addon panel.

User Interface

The plugin panel is located in the sidebar of the 3D Viewport (press N key to open). Find it under the Piano tab.

Main Settings Panel

Key Settings

  • White Key Prefix: Name prefix for white keys

  • Black Key Prefix: Name prefix for black keys

  • Key Separator: Separator character for key names

  • White Key Color: Default color for white keys

  • Black Key Color: Default color for black keys

Hammer Settings

  • Hammer Prefix: Name prefix for hammers

  • Hammer Movement: Movement distance for hammers

Pedal Settings

  • Damper Pedal: Object name for damper pedal

  • Sostenuto Pedal: Object name for sostenuto pedal

  • Una Corda Pedal: Object name for una corda pedal

  • Pedal Rotation: Rotation angle for pedals

Workflow

Preparation

  • Ensure the piano model uses the correct object naming.

  • Use the Test Components button to verify correct mappings.

Creating Animation

  1. Click the Create Piano Animation button.

  2. Select the MIDI file to import.

  3. Wait for the animation to be generated.

Adjust Parameters

  • Fine-tune emission effects as needed.

  • Adjust key colors and animation settings.

  • Preview results in real-time.

Important Notes

Piano Model Requirements

  • 52 white keys

  • 36 black keys

  • 88 hammers

  • 3 pedals

Naming Conventions

  • White Keys: meshId57_name.1 to meshId57_name.52

  • Black Keys: meshId56_name.1 to meshId56_name.36

  • Hammers: pCube1 to pCube88

  • Pedals: piano_pedal_metal01, piano_pedal_metal02, piano_pedal_metal03

Performance Considerations

  • Larger MIDI files take longer to generate animations.

  • It is recommended to test with shorter MIDI files first.

Troubleshooting

If component testing fails

  • Check if object naming follows the required conventions.

  • Confirm that all necessary piano components are present.

  • Verify the object hierarchy is correct.

If animation effects are unsatisfactory

  • Modify the movement settings for keys and hammers.

  • Check and adjust pedal rotation settings.

Technical Support

  • Version: 1.0

  • Category: Animation

  • Contact: [qq475519905]
